title: Your company just got flagged for a SOC 2 audit and the auditor asks you to prove which model version served predictions to customers on a specific date three months ago. What do you need in place to answer this in minutes instead of days?
prompt:
Your company just got flagged for a SOC 2 audit and the auditor asks you to prove which model version served predictions to customers on a specific date three months ago. What do you need in place to answer this in minutes instead of days?

*Answer in the editor as you would out loud in an interview (a few sentences to a short paragraph is fine). Then use 💡 Hint, 🧠 AI feedback, or 📗 Show answer.*
reference_solution:
You need structured, queryable inference-level logging that ties every prediction back to a model version, the exact feature values used, and the code commit that produced it, stored somewhere append-only and indexed so you can filter by date and customer in seconds. Every inference call should emit a record with model version, feature snapshot, code commit SHA, timestamp, and request ID, and the registry itself needs to track exactly which version was live at which time window.

**Takeaway:** "Prove it in minutes" only works if the answer was designed to be queryable from day one — if your only option is grepping through scattered application logs, you don't have an audit trail, you have exhaust that happens to contain the answer somewhere.
hint_notes: Every inference call needs to emit an append-only, indexed record: model version, feature snapshot, code commit SHA, timestamp, request ID. The registry must track which version was live in which time window.
